subject: Physics
title: Quantum Biology
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Quantum Biology.

hook.text: Plants may use quantum mechanics to photosynthesize. Evolution might have discovered quantum tricks first.

buildup.text: Photosynthesis transfers energy with near-perfect efficiency. Classical physics can't fully explain how.

discovery.text: Studies suggest energy explores multiple paths simultaneously through quantum coherence.

twist.text: Birds may navigate using quantum entanglement in their eyes to sense Earth's magnetic field.

climax.text: If confirmed, quantum biology means life evolved to exploit physics' strangest features.

punchline.text: Plants might be quantum computers. Nature got there first.

quiz.question: How might plants use quantum mechanics?
options: Quantum coherence helps photosynthesis transfer energy efficiently
They split atoms in their cells
Leaves create quantum superposition of colors
